text: Hi, welcome to our booth. We are Group 106, and we partnered with Murphy Oil, a U.S.-based upstream oil and gas company.
Our capstone looks at their inspection data to answer one practical question: what is really driving corrosion in their equipment?
We focused on horizontal separators and the piping circuits connected to them.
A horizontal separator is a large vessel that takes the production stream and separates it into oil, gas, and water,
so each stream can move on for further refining and distribution.
To identify the corrosion drivers, we started with manual analysis, using the literature to map the mechanisms we would expect to see.
Then we took that same inspection data and trained machine learning models on it.
We fed the models hundreds of variables and asked a simple question: can they predict percent wall loss in the piping?
Across the models, we reached about 86% accuracy, with an average error of 2.6%.
In practical terms, that means the predicted wall-loss percentage is usually within about 3% of the actual value.
That might still sound high, but for a problem with this many interacting factors, those are strong results.
Machine learning helps here because it lets us use the full data set at once, instead of hand-testing every factor.
The models can spot patterns that are hard to see by eye.
Then we open the models back up and examine which relationships matter most,
rather than manually working through thousands of possible parameter combinations, one by one.
Once we had those correlations, we compared them with historical failure events.
At a high level, those failure records supported our main trends, including the regions and circuits most susceptible to corrosion.
But some of the more specific insights could not be confirmed, because the failure records did not include enough detail.
Finally, we brought those insights into a Power BI dashboard that lets Murphy Oil engineers explore the patterns and correlations for themselves.
It updates automatically whenever new inspection data comes in.
It also risk-ranks the equipment locations that may need more frequent inspection or closer attention,
based on a formula we created using the validated parameters we identified.
